research_topic: AI monetization reset and model-agnostic platform hedge
current_action: 持有 / 觀望，等待下一次驗證點
latest_delta: Microsoft earnings press release available on Investor Relations website
Microsoft Cloud and AI strength drives third quarter results
Microsoft announces quarterly earnings release date
noise_filters: 單一功能 rollback 未連到企業端 adoption
Macro headline without Azure or Copilot impact
Short-term price weakness without revision in demand

thesis.statement: 微軟仍是 AI 基礎設施與企業分發的核心受益者，但要讓股價脫離 CapEx 懷疑，必須用 Copilot adoption、M365 漲價與 Azure AI 需求證明其估值可以重新從成熟雲端股回到高品質 AI 平台股。
thesis.core_catalyst: Copilot adoption、M365 pricing pass-through、Azure AI demand.
thesis.market_blind_spot: 市場短線聚焦高 CapEx，低估 model-agnostic Copilot 與商業訂價權恢復帶來的 EPS 扭轉。
thesis.expiry_condition: Azure growth falls below 30%, Copilot adoption remains stalled, or flagship government cloud access materially weakens.

assumptions.statement: Azure AI demand can keep total Azure growth above 30%.
assumptions.verification_method: Quarterly Azure growth and AI contribution disclosures.
assumptions.invalidation_condition: Azure growth drops below 30% or AI contribution decelerates materially.

assumptions.statement: Copilot adoption can improve enough to justify the AI distribution premium.
assumptions.verification_method: Enterprise adoption commentary, seat growth, and attach rates.
assumptions.invalidation_condition: Copilot stays below 10% enterprise penetration through the next earnings cycle.

assumptions.statement: CapEx growth will translate into durable revenue and margin support rather than pure cost drag.
assumptions.verification_method: CapEx trend, gross margin trend, and monetization updates.
assumptions.invalidation_condition: CapEx keeps accelerating while margin and monetization do not improve for two quarters.

risks.statement: CapEx remains a black hole and the market strips out the AI premium.
risks.response: Reduce tactical exposure and re-rate on mature-software multiples.

risks.statement: Copilot remains productively underwhelming and adoption stalls.
risks.response: Exit the AI monetization thesis and keep only core cloud exposure if needed.

risks.statement: Government or regulatory friction hurts model-neutral cloud strategy.
risks.response: Trim if government cloud narrative weakens the platform hedge.

valuation_regime.current_yardstick: Mature cloud P/E plus AI premium
valuation_regime.better_yardstick: AI platform and enterprise distribution premium
valuation_regime.switch_trigger: Copilot monetization shows real seat growth and CapEx intensity stabilizes.
valuation_regime.re_rating_logic: The market pays again for AI distribution and pricing power instead of just infrastructure spend.
valuation_regime.associated_risk: If monetization lags, the premium disappears faster than fundamentals deteriorate.

scenarios.trigger: Azure stays strong and Copilot improves modestly by next earnings.
scenarios.logic: The thesis stays alive but valuation remains range-bound until proof arrives.
scenarios.action: Hold and wait for earnings confirmation before adding.

scenarios.trigger: Copilot adoption inflects and M365 pricing flows through cleanly.
scenarios.logic: MSFT is re-rated from capex-heavy cloud operator to AI productivity platform.
scenarios.action: Add after confirmation on numbers, not before.

scenarios.trigger: CapEx keeps rising while Copilot remains stuck.
scenarios.logic: AI premium compresses and the market defaults to mature-software multiples.
scenarios.action: Trim into strength and protect capital.

scenarios.trigger: Azure growth breaks below 30% or Copilot monetization clearly fails.
scenarios.logic: The core causal chain is broken.

action_rules.condition: Copilot enterprise penetration breaks above 10% and CapEx growth moderates.
CapEx rises again while Copilot monetization does not improve.
action_rules.action: Trim into strength and lower conviction.

action_rules.condition: Azure growth falls below 30% or flagship cloud access is materially impaired.
action_rules.action: Exit active thesis and rotate to better risk/reward.

next_must_check_data: FY2026 Q3 earnings: Azure growth, AI contribution, Copilot monetization, margin trend, and CapEx guidance.
research_debt: Need a cleaner measure of paid Copilot seats versus trial activity.
Need a better read on government cloud exposure to model-sourcing disputes.

version_log.reason: Baseline migration from legacy MSFT notes and March update.
version_log.impact: Established living thesis and machine-readable state.
Automatic fallback refresh used. Review the generated diff before merging.

seed_events.title: Q2 FY2026 Azure stayed strong but CapEx nearly reached $30B.
Copilot enterprise penetration remained near 3%, below expectations.
